name: Real-Time Ingestion
description: Collaborative processing of incoming information as it arrives

content.title: Real-Time Ingestion: Collaborative Processing
content.content:
# Real-Time Ingestion: Collaborative Processing

## Email as Primary Input Channel

In everyday Middle Office work, most new information arrives via email. Brokers send submissions, cedents report claims, and counterparties exchange documentation—all through email with attachments.

Real-time ingestion captures this flow as it happens, with AI agents and human users working together to process and classify incoming information.

The key distinction from historic ingestion is that **the user is an active participant**, not just a validator.

## Email Integration Options

### Option 1: Dedicated Mailbox Monitoring

A dedicated mailbox (e.g., claims@company.com) receives incoming communications. An AI agent monitors continuously, processing each email as it arrives.

When an email arrives, the agent:
- Extracts the email body and parses attachments
- Identifies the sender and maps to known counterparties
- Classifies the email type (claim notification, submission, inquiry)
- Extracts key data fields based on classification
- Creates draft structured records for user review

**Advantages:**
- Centralized processing with complete capture
- Consistent handling regardless of which user receives the email
- Clear audit trail from email receipt to processed record

### Option 2: Outlook Plugin (Dynamics-Style)

Users process emails from their own inboxes using an Outlook plugin. When viewing an email, the user clicks a "Process" button.

The agent returns:
- Suggested classification and categorization
- Extracted data fields ready for review
- Proposed links to existing records
- Draft updates to document management

**Advantages:**
- User-controlled processing
- Selective ingestion—not every email needs AI processing
- Integration with existing workflow habits

## The Collaborative Workflow

Regardless of approach, real-time ingestion is fundamentally collaborative:

1. **Agent Processing**: Extract content, classify, propose data mapping
2. **User Collaboration**: Review proposals, correct, complete missing information
3. **Approval**: User approves submission
4. **Output**: Data Mesh (connected records), Structured Data (claims, submissions), Document Storage

## PoC Approach: Curated Dataset

The PoC will **not** connect to live mailboxes. Instead, we work with a curated dataset of representative emails exported from production. This allows demonstration of the full collaborative workflow without requiring production email access.
